title: Handling resumption of transmissions and lbt performance in 3gpp multi-carrier elaa, license-assisted access
abstract: Handling uplink transmissions by UEs in unlicensed carrier components, in the context of 3GPP eLAA, enhanced license assisted access, and in particular, the configuration by the eNodeB of multi-carrier LBT, Listen Before Transmit, is discussed. The eNodeB signals together with the Uplink grant the LBT procedure to be used before transmitting in uplink, either a short, Category 2 LBT procedure, or else a longer, category 4 LBT procedure. The eNodeB signals, for a particular carrier component, the same LBT procedure it has used itself to sense and then securing the carrier. Also, when pauses occur during the uplink transmission on contention-based shared channels involved in eLAA communications, various procedures may be used to restart transmissions on the contention-based shared channels on the uplink when the original channel occupancy was reserved by a serving base station.
claims:
Claims [00144] WHAT IS CLAIMED IS:  CLAIMS 1. A method of wireless communication, comprising: receiving a resource allocation for a contiguous transmission over a set of subframes on a secondary carrier including contention-based shared spectrum; initiating transmission on the secondary carrier; pausing transmission on the secondary carrier prior to completion of the contiguous transmission over the set of subframes of the resource allocation; resuming transmission of the contiguous transmission on the secondary carrier after the pausing in response to a successful full listen before talk (LBT) check, wherein the resuming reoccupies the secondary carrier without start of a new channel occupancy of the secondary carrier. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the pausing the transmission includes: dropping the transmission based on a power-limited mode of operation. 3. The method of claim 1, further including: detecting activity on the secondary carrier during the pausing. 4. The method of claim 3, further including: detecting no activity on the secondary carrier during the pausing; and resuming transmission of the contiguous transmission on the secondary carrier after the pausing when the no activity is detected in response to a successful reduced LBT check, wherein the resuming after the pausing when the no activity is detected reoccupies the secondary carrier without the start of the new channel occupancy of the secondary carrier. 5. The method of claim 1, further including: receiving an LBT type indicator from a serving base station, wherein the type indicator identifies a type of LBT procedure performed by the serving base station for securing the secondary carrier for allocation of the contiguous transmission, and wherein the resuming the transmission is in response to an LBT procedure performed according to the LBT type indicator received from the serving base station. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the type of LBT procedure includes one of: a full LBT check; or a reduced LBT check. 7. The method of claim 5, further including: detecting activity on the carrier during the pausing. 8. The method of claim 7, further including: ignoring the LBT type indicator when the LBT type indicator identifies a reduced LBT check performed by the serving base station, in response to the detecting the activity. 9. A method of wireless communication, comprising: receiving an uplink grant at a user equipment (UE) from a base station in a multi- carrier transmission network with contention-based shared spectrum; receiving a listen before talk (LBT) type indicator at the UE from the base station, wherein the LBT type indicator identifies a type of LBT procedure performed by the base station; detecting, by the UE, a transmission gap less than a predefined length on a contention- based shared carrier associated with the uplink grant; failing a first reduced LBT procedure at the UE for the contention-based shared carrier; performing a second LBT procedure at the UE in accordance with the LBT type indicator identifying a full LBT procedure performed by the base station; and dismissing, by the UE, a transmission attempt associated with the uplink grant in response to the LBT type indicator identifying a reduced LBT procedure performed by the base station. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein the detecting the transmission gap includes: dropping the transmission for the transmission gap based on a power- limited mode of operation of the UE. 11. The method of claim 9, further including: detecting no activity on the contention-based shared carrier during the transmission gap- 12. An apparatus configured for wireless communication, the apparatus comprising: at least one processor; and a memory coupled to the at least one processor, wherein the at least one processor is configured to: receive a resource allocation for a contiguous transmission over a set of subframes on a secondary carrier including contention-based shared spectrum; initiate transmission on the secondary carrier; pause transmission on the secondary carrier prior to completion of the contiguous transmission over the set of subframes of the resource allocation; resume transmission of the contiguous transmission on the secondary carrier after the pause of the transmission in response to a successful full listen before talk (LBT) check, wherein resuming transmission reoccupies the secondary carrier without start of a new channel occupancy of the secondary carrier. 13. The apparatus of claim 12, wherein the configuration of the at least one processor to pause the transmission includes configuration of the at least one processor to: drop the transmission based on a power-limited mode of operation. 14. The apparatus of claim 12, further including configuration of the at least one processor to: detect activity on the secondary carrier during the pause of the transmission. 15. The apparatus of claim 14, further including configuration of the at least one processor to: detect no activity on the secondary carrier during the pause of the transmission; and resume transmission of the contiguous transmission on the secondary carrier after the pause of the transmission when the no activity is detected in response to a successful reduced LBT check, wherein resuming the transmission after the pause of the transmission when the no activity is detected reoccupies the secondary carrier without the start of the new channel occupancy of the secondary carrier. 16. The apparatus of claim 12, further including configuration of the at least one processor to: receive an LBT type indicator from a serving base station, wherein the type indicator identifies a type of LBT procedure performed by the serving base station for securing the secondary carrier for allocation of the contiguous transmission, and wherein resuming the transmission the transmission is in response to an LBT procedure performed according to the LBT type indicator received from the serving base station. 17. The apparatus of claim 16, wherein the type of LBT procedure includes one of: a full LBT check; or a reduced LBT check. 18. The apparatus of claim 16, further including configuration of the at least one processor to: detect activity on the carrier during the pause of the transmission. 19. The apparatus of claim 18, further including configuration of the at least one processor to: ignore the LBT type indicator when the LBT type indicator identifies a reduced LBT check performed by the serving base station, in response to detecting the activity. 20. An apparatus configured for wireless communication, the apparatus comprising: at least one processor; and a memory coupled to the at least one processor, wherein the at least one processor is configured to: receive an uplink grant at a user equipment (UE) from a base station in a multi-carrier transmission network with contention-based shared spectrum; receive a listen before talk (LBT) type indicator at the UE from the base station, wherein the LBT type indicator identifies a type of LBT procedure performed by the base station; detect, by the UE, a transmission gap less than a predefined length on a contention-based shared carrier associated with the uplink grant; 

 fail a first reduced LBT procedure at the UE for the contention-based shared carrier; perform a second LBT procedure at the UE in accordance with the LBT type indicator identifying a full LBT procedure performed by the base station; and dismiss, by the UE, a transmission attempt associated with the uplink grant in response to the LBT type indicator identifying a reduced LBT procedure performed by the base station. 21. The apparatus of claim 20, wherein the configuration of the at least one processor to detect the transmission gap includes configuration of the at least one processor to: drop the transmission for the transmission gap based on a power-limited mode of operation of the UE. 22. The apparatus of claim 20, further including configuration of the at least one processor to: detect no activity on the contention-based shared carrier during the transmission gap.
